fields.description:
## Why
The gate critiques prompt hygiene (T8) but its own reviewer prompts accumulate DO-NOT constraints. One eval-guarded pass applies that standard to itself.

## What (with review-surfaced corrections)
- Scope = the 41 src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md files (+ the shared pass prompts). (The broader ~59 count includes code-review reviewers, out of scope here.)
- R-6 organizing principle: lead with affirmative specification; keep needed prohibitions terse with an adjacent "do this instead"; do not narrate failure mechanics. Add a review-time checklist habit; STATE where it lives (CONTRIBUTING.md or docs/plan-review-gate.md).
- G-12 prompt-injection directive. CORRECTION: there is NO existing shared-scaffolding / preamble-composition layer -- resolve_prompt loads each prompt whole. So this story must FIRST introduce a shared-preamble composition point (in passes.py prompt resolution), then place the directive there; otherwise it degrades to per-prompt duplication.
- FP-3(c) forward-looking rule: hoist to the shared preamble. CORRECTION: it lives ONLY in plan_review_F1.md today (E1 does not carry it).
- Back-out: revert the prompt-edit commit; document it (T4/T9 reversibility).

## Provenance
Labels like G-8 / FP-3a / R-6 / WS-N are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020) -- they are NOT codebase symbols and cannot be grepped. Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shared-preamble composition point introduced; injection directive + forward-looking rule live there once. Proof: unit test asserts every plan_review reviewer resolves with the shared preamble prepended.
- [ ] Affirmative-framing sweep complete over the 41 files. Proof: a checklist run recorded per file (count = 41, zero remaining bare DO-NOT-only blocks) in the ticket.
- [ ] Eval/sidecar regression: reworded prompts keep prior recall. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` pass-rate >= pre-sweep baseline (record both numbers). If it regresses: revert.
- [ ] Review-time checklist location documented.
